name: Duck Curry - Kaen of Duck
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp Minced Cilantro Root (or Extra Cilantro Leaves)
1 tsp Grated Lime or Lemon Peel
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the duck, dry thoroughly, and cut into serving pieces. Set aside.
2. In a deep saucepan or casserole, bring the coconut milk to a boil. Simmer for 5 minutes. Pour off half and set aside. Then simmer the remaining milk for about 15 minutes. Add the duck and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
3. Drain the chilies and pound with salt to create a paste. Grind peppercorns, caraway seeds, and coriander seeds together. Pound these with the chilies to form a smooth paste. Add dried shrimp paste, cilantro root or leaves, lemon grass, lime, shallots, crushed garlic, and minced ginger. Combine all ingredients into a smooth paste.
4. Stir the seasoning paste into the duck and coconut milk mixture, adding only half at first. Taste before adding the remaining seasoning paste. Pour in the reserved coconut milk, a generous amount of fish sauce, and sweet basil leaves. Simmer until the duck is cooked through and the sauce thickens and becomes flavorful.
5. Check the seasoning and serve with rice. Unused seasoning paste can be stored for several days in a covered jar in the refrigerator.
